meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/microsoft-365 + learn.microsoft.com/entra) — portal paths and step wording grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03.
generalNotes: These are tenant-wide org settings, not per-user objects — one change applies to the whole tenant. There is nothing to stage in Report-only, so validate by re-reading the setting after saving.

Two of the three live under Microsoft 365 admin center > Settings > Org Settings (different tabs); the guest-access one lives in the Microsoft Entra admin center. The exact path is on each policy below.

Role needed: Password expiration = User Administrator; Guest access restriction = User Administrator (Global Admin also works); Self-service trials/purchases = Global Administrator only (including partners acting AOBO).

requiredSettings.label: Set passwords to never expire (recommended)
requiredSettings.value: Checked / enabled — this is the target baseline state (passwordsNeverExpire = true)

requiredSettings.label: Days before passwords expire
requiredSettings.value: N/A — this field is only used if you clear the never-expire box (valid range 14–730)

requiredSettings.label: Scope
requiredSettings.value: Cloud-only accounts. Passwords synced from on-prem AD via Entra Connect follow the on-prem AD DS policy, not this setting.

steps: Sign in to the Microsoft 365 admin center as a User Administrator.
Left nav → Show all → Settings → Org Settings.
Open the Security & Privacy tab → select Password expiration policy.
Ensure 'Set passwords to never expire (recommended)' is checked, then Save. (If it is already checked, the baseline is met — no change needed.)
Deliver alongside MFA enforcement and communicate to users, since anyone used to periodic expiry prompts will stop receiving them (expiry notifications have also been retired by Microsoft).

Note: never-expire passwords still age on LastPasswordChangeDateTime — if this is ever switched back to expiring, any password older than 90 days forces a change at next sign-in. PowerShell equivalent: Update-MgUser -UserId <id> -PasswordPolicies DisablePasswordExpiration.

requiredSettings.label: Guest user access
requiredSettings.value: Guest user access is restricted to properties and memberships of their own directory objects (most restrictive)

requiredSettings.label: Effect
requiredSettings.value: Guests can see only their own profile — no other users, no groups, no group memberships (even when searching by UPN or object ID).

requiredSettings.label: Propagation
requiredSettings.value: Change can take up to ~15 minutes to take effect for guest users.

steps: Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center (entra.microsoft.com) as a User Administrator.
Browse to Entra ID → External Identities → External collaboration settings.
Under Guest user access, select 'Guest user access is restricted to properties and memberships of their own directory objects (most restrictive)'.
Select Save. Allow up to 15 minutes for it to apply.

Note: this restricts directory read only. It does NOT block a guest's access to joined groups in Microsoft 365 services like Teams, and guests can still be added to admin roles regardless — those are governed separately (Conditional Access / role assignment).

requiredSettings.label: Per-product setting
requiredSettings.value: Do not allow — set on each eligible product (there is NO single tenant-wide off switch; the policy is managed per product)

requiredSettings.label: Options per product
requiredSettings.value: Allow | Allow for trials only | Do not allow. Baseline = Do not allow for every product.

requiredSettings.label: Role
requiredSettings.value: Global Administrator only (also required for partners acting AOBO).

requiredSettings.label: Propagation
requiredSettings.value: Up to 72 hours for a change to take effect. New products Microsoft adds later default to Allow, so re-check periodically.

steps: Sign in to the Microsoft 365 admin center as a Global Administrator.
Settings → Org Settings → Services tab → Self-service trials and purchases.
The page lists every eligible product and its current setting. Select a product name to open its panel.
Set it to 'Do not allow' and Save changes. Repeat for each product in the list (this is why the baseline covers all ~28).

Faster bulk alternative via PowerShell (MSCommerce module): review with `Get-MSCommerceProductPolicies -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase`, then for each ProductId run `Update-MSCommerceProductPolicy -PolicyId AllowSelfServicePurchase -ProductId <ProductId> -Enabled $False`.

Note: AllowSelfServicePurchase covers both purchases and trials in one setting, and admins keep the ability to centrally assign licensed products — only the user-initiated self-serve path is blocked. The product list changes over time, so the exact count can drift from 28; verify against the current list rather than the number.
